_meta.name: UI Coherence Auditor Spec
_meta.purpose: Machine-readable audit model for detecting interface coherence failure and signal-to-noise collapse across product surfaces.

umbrellaProblem.definition: A product-quality failure where a screen exposes more competing structure, controls, labels, and persistent text than the task can justify, while failing to compose them into one legible product surface.

dimensions.label: Composition Integrity
dimensions.definition: Whether headers, controls, labels, and actions read as one composed unit rather than bolted-together fragments.
signals: unclear ownership between label and value
exampleRules: flag when more than one header control targets the same scope dimension
flag when more than one primary-looking action appears in the same control band

dimensions.label: Hierarchy Clarity
dimensions.definition: Whether the screen clearly communicates primary task, secondary context, and tertiary metadata.
signals: multiple primary-looking regions above the fold
primary surface visually smaller than surrounding chrome

dimensions.label: Semantic Economy
dimensions.definition: Whether the UI avoids duplicate meaning across labels, statuses, helper text, and controls.
signals: duplicate domain nouns in one region
multiple controls bound to one semantic job
repeated copy with low informational delta

dimensions.label: Surface Economy
dimensions.definition: Whether visible structure is justified by the task instead of inflated by container, card, and chip bloat.
signals: too many containers above the fold
too many summary surfaces relative to action objects
too many decorative labels and chips

dimensions.label: Interaction Appropriateness
dimensions.definition: Whether controls reduce work instead of forcing unnecessary manual decisions.
signals: controls repeating route or role defaults
manual steps the system should infer

dimensions.label: Design System Conformance
dimensions.definition: Whether spacing, type, sizing, radius, and component treatments remain consistent with approved system rules.
signals: mixed control variants for same role
inconsistent typography for same semantic level

dimensions.label: Product Language Quality
dimensions.definition: Whether visible copy sounds productized and task-oriented instead of internal, placeholder-like, or mechanically generated.

dimensions.label: Scanability and Density Discipline
dimensions.definition: Whether the screen is easy to scan without overload, mixed density, or fragmented attention.
signals: too many focal points above the fold

levels.definition: Blocks shipping. Causes direct task confusion, contradictory meaning, strong product-language leakage, or severe bloat.
Should block merge unless explicitly waived. Meaningfully harms clarity, speed, or trust.
Should be fixed in the next pass. Adds real friction but is not immediately blocking alone.
Visible issue with lower structural impact.

ruleCandidates.logic: Two or more visible controls in the same header region target the same scope or time dimension.
examples: Status dropdown + status chip group for the same job

ruleCandidates.logic: Helper text overlaps heavily with adjacent label or obvious UI state and adds no decision value.
User-facing copy includes internal, implementation, QA, or schema-sounding phrasing.
Container, card, chip, or summary count exceeds a defined threshold before the primary task surface begins.
Spacing, sizing, or radius values deviate from approved token scales without exemption.
The control pattern adds manual work or choice overhead relative to the task and option count.

preferred: surface context: primary actor, task, object
analytics for usage and control effect
